questions.question: Choose the correct meaning of the idiom: ”Break the ice.”
questions.explanation: To break the ice means to start a conversation or ease initial awkwardness.

questions.question: Select the option that best completes the sentence: ”Had I known about the meeting earlier, I attended it.”
questions.explanation: The correct conditional form is: Had I known about the meeting earlier, I would have attended it.

questions.question: Identify the option where the underlined phrase is used incorrectly: ”She is interested on learning new languages.”
questions.explanation: The incorrect phrase is interested on; the correct usage is interested in.

questions.question: Pick the sentence that best conveys the meaning of the phrase ”To kill two birds with one stone.”
options: To cause trouble for many people
To achieve two goals with a single action
To waste time on unnecessary things
To be unable to decide between two options
questions.explanation: To kill two birds with one stone means to achieve two goals with a single action.

questions.question: Choose the correct meaning of the phrase ”Throw down the gauntlet.”
questions.explanation: To throw down the gauntlet means to challenge someone.

questions.question: Rearrange the following parts to form a meaningful sentence: A) despite the heavy rain B) the match continued C) and the players showed great determination D) yesterday
questions.explanation: The best sequence is: yesterday, despite the heavy rain, the match continued and the players showed great determination.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the most appropriate word: The politician’s speech was full of designed to win over the crowd without saying much of substance.
questions.explanation: Rhetoric means persuasive language, often with more style than substance.

questions.question: Rearrange the parts to form a logical sentence: A) the city’s new policy B) has significantly improved C) air quality D) over the past year
questions.explanation: The logical sentence is: the city’s new policy has significantly improved air quality over the past year.

questions.question: Identify the option that best replaces the underlined word: Her argument was specious, convincing but false.
questions.explanation: Specious means seemingly convincing but actually misleading or false.
